title: Making AI Explainable

modules.title: Black Box Problem
modules.sub: Unpacking why AI decisions are often invisible and opaque
modules.description: Learners examine how complex AI models obscure their internal reasoning and why this creates risk in professional contexts. They assess real-world scenarios where lack of transparency has led to flawed or contested decisions, building a clear case for why explainability matters.

modules.title: Interpretation Techniques
modules.sub: Applying practical tools to surface AI model reasoning
modules.description: Learners work through established explainability methods such as LIME, SHAP, and saliency mapping to interpret model outputs. They apply these techniques to example models, comparing their strengths and limitations so they can select the right approach for different use cases.

modules.title: Transparency in Decision Making
modules.sub: Embedding explainability standards into AI-driven workflows
modules.description: Learners evaluate how transparency requirements vary across industries, regulations, and stakeholder groups. They design decision-making frameworks that incorporate explainability checkpoints, ensuring AI outputs can be audited and justified within their own organizational context.

modules.title: Building Trust Through Explanation
modules.sub: Communicating AI rationale clearly to diverse human audiences
modules.description: Learners craft explanations of AI decisions tailored to different audiences, from technical teams to executives and end users. They practice translating complex model behavior into accessible narratives and evaluate how consistent, honest explanation builds long-term stakeholder trust in AI systems.

synopsis: Understand why many AI systems are "black boxes" and how to make them more transparent. Learn techniques for explaining AI decisions to humans.
